etymology.description: 推(推) (tuī, "push") and 拉(拉) (lā, "pull") are combined directly to express the reciprocal actions of physically exerting force on an object away from and towards a person or center, respectively.
etymology.interpretation: 推(推) (tuī, "push") involves exerting force to move something away, while 拉(拉) (lā, "pull") involves drawing something closer by force. In combination, 推拉 describes the dual physical actions or motions often encountered in daily activities, such as opening doors or operating machinery that requires both pushing and pulling actions.

examples.chinese: 推拉门很方便(推拉門很方便)。
请勿用力推拉这扇门(請勿用力推拉這扇門)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng wù yòng lì tuīlā zhè shàn mén.
examples.english: Please do not forcefully push and pull this door.

examples.chinese: 这个游戏需要团队的推拉合作(這個遊戲需要團隊的推拉合作)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège yóuxì xūyào tuánduì de tuīlā hézuò.
examples.english: This game requires push and pull cooperation from the team.
